summarization.a: The figure displays absorbance spectra for different samples labeled AIO - PO (1-6), AIO - PO (1-3), and AIO - PO (1-1) across a range of wavenumbers.

a.question: In what FTIR region is the P-O bond typically found?
a.answer: There are two regions: between 950 and 1300 cm⁻¹, and between 400 and 550 cm⁻¹.

a.question: In what FTIR region are the Al-O-P and Al-O bonds typically found?
How many different samples are shown here?
What change in the FTIR spectra indicates increased phosphorus incorporation when moving from the 1-1 to the 1-3 and 1-6 supercycle processes?
a.answer: Figure 8 shows that increasing the number of PO cycles leads to a pronounced increase in the intensity of bands associated with P–O interactions, most notably the peaks around 1000 and 1200 cm⁻¹, which are attributed to P–O–P bonding. Simultaneously, the intensity of bands related to Al–O and Al–O–P bonds decreases, reflecting the lower relative aluminum content. Together, these spectral changes indicate a transition from a P-doped aluminum oxide toward an ortho- or polyphosphate material.
